content.text:
`workflowApiClient` is **defined but not actually used** anywhere in the codebase.

It's defined in:
- `lib/api/workflowApiClient.ts` — exports `getWorkflowApiClient()` and `fetchJobsFromWorkflow()` helpers

However, the actual workflow API calls use different approaches:

1. **`app/api/jobs/route.ts`** — Uses native `fetch` directly with `WORKFLOW_API_KEY` header (server-side proxy route)
2. **`lib/api/batch.ts`** — Uses the standard `fetcher` utility to call `/api/jobs` (which hits the proxy route above)

The `workflowApiClient` appears to be **dead code** — it was likely created as a client-side Axios-based helper but the project settled on using the server-side proxy pattern (`app/api/jobs/route.ts`) instead, which keeps the API key server-side and uses standard `fetch`.
remove it

+| Variable                            | Purpose                                               |
+| ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL`          | External API base (includes `/api/v1`)                |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRODUCT_API_BASE_URL`  | Product API endpoint                                  |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_BASE_URL` | Workflow engine API endpoint (build-time)             |
+| `WORKFLOW_API_KEY`                  | Workflow engine API key (runtime, from K8s secret)    |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_ID`    | Keycloak client                                       |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_KEYCLOAK_ISSUER`       | Keycloak server URL                                   |
+| `NEXTAUTH_SECRET`                   | NextAuth secret (also used as Keycloak client secret) |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV`               | Environment identifier (development, qa, production)  |
+
+### Workflow Engine Integration
+
+Workflow engine integration follows the pattern established in mes-genai-ui:
+
+**Configuration:**
+
+- `NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_BASE_URL` — Build-time variable, baked into bundle
+- `WORKFLOW_API_KEY` — Runtime variable, injected via Kubernetes secrets
+
+**Environment-specific URLs:**
+
+- Dev: `https://workflow-management-dev.makeitmes.com`
+- QA: `https://workflow-management-qa.makeitmes.com`
+- Prod: `https://workflow-management.makeitmes.com`
+
+**API Client (`lib/api/workflowApiClient.ts`):**
+
+- Uses Axios with lazy initialization
+- API key injected at request time via interceptor (not baked at build)
+- Header: `x-api-key`
+- Server-side proxy at `app/api/jobs/route.ts` for server-to-server calls
+
+### Multi-Environment Setup
+
+**Build-time environment selection:**
+
+```bash
+# Build script selects correct env file
+./set-environment.sh [dev|qa|prod]
+```
+
+**Environment files:**
+
+- `env/.env.dev` — Development configuration
+- `env/.env.qa` — QA configuration
+- `env/.env.prod` — Production configuration
+
+The `set-environment.sh` script copies the appropriate env file to `.env.production` during Docker build based on `BUILD_ENVIRONMENT` argument.
+
+### Helm Deployment
+
+**Chart location:** `styls-admin-ui/`
+
+**Key templates:**
+
+- `deployment.yaml` — App deployment with WORKFLOW_API_KEY from secret
+- `external-secrets.yaml` — External Secrets Operator config for WORKFLOW_API_KEY
+- `tls-external-secret.yaml` — TLS certificate management
+- `ingress.yaml` — NGINX ingress configuration
+
+**Secret Management:**
+
+- Uses External Secrets Operator to sync from cloud secret managers (AWS Secrets Manager / GCP Secret Manager)
+- `WORKFLOW_API_KEY` synced from `workflow-engine-api-key-{env}` secret
+- Refresh interval: 24 hours
+
+**Environment values:**
+
+- `values/values-dev.yaml` — Dev overrides (replicas: 1)
+- `values/values-qa.yaml` — QA overrides (replicas: 1)
+- `values/values-prod.yaml` — Prod overrides (HPA enabled, node affinity)

content.text:
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1beta1
kind: ExternalSecret
metadata:
  name: {{ include "styls-admin-ui.fullname" . }}-workflow-secrets
  labels:
    {{- include "styls-admin-ui.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
spec:
  secretStoreRef:
    name: {{ .Values.workflow.secretStore | default .Values.secretStore }}
    kind: ClusterSecretStore
  target:
    name: {{ include "styls-admin-ui.fullname" . }}-workflow-secrets
    creationPolicy: Owner
    template:
      data:
        WORKFLOW_API_KEY: "{{ `{{ .workflow_api_key | toString }}` }}"
  data:
    - secretKey: workflow_api_key
      remoteRef:
        key: {{ .Values.workflow.secretName }}
        property: {{ .Values.workflow.secretProperty | default "workflow_engine_api_key" }}
  refreshInterval: {{ .Values.workflow.secretRefreshInterval | default "24h" }}

content.text: Found several FIXME comments that are outdated and can be cleaned up. Also need to update AGENTS.md since it still references the deleted `workflowApiClient.ts`.

# AGENTS.md

This file provides guidance to AI agents when working with code in this repository.

## Commands

```bash
npm run dev          # Development server (Turbopack)
npm run build        # Production build
npm run lint         # ESLint
npm run format       # Prettier format
npm run format:check # Check formatting without writing
npm test             # Vitest tests
npm run test:ui      # Vitest with UI
npm run coverage     # Test coverage
```

## Architecture

Next.js 15 (App Router) admin UI for styls.ai. Uses React 19, TanStack Query v5, NextAuth v4 (Keycloak), Tailwind v4, Radix UI, and Sonner for toasts.

### Key directories

- `app/` — Pages and API proxy routes
- `app/api/` — Server-side routes that proxy to external backend with auth
- `lib/api/` — Client-side React Query hooks and types per domain (batch, product, orders, user, workflow)
- `lib/auth/options.ts` — NextAuth Keycloak config; JWT carries `accessToken`, `idToken`, `roles`
- `lib/config.ts` — `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRODUCT_API_BASE_URL` helpers
- `lib/utils/environment.ts` — Environment detection helpers (`isDevelopment()`, `isQAEnvironment()`, `isProduction()`)
- `components/` — Shared UI components built on Radix UI primitives

### Data fetching pattern

All API calls flow through `lib/api/fetcher.ts`:

- `fetcher(url, options)` — returns `Response`; automatically signs out and shows a toast on 401
- `fetchJson<T>(url, options)` — returns `T`; throws with parsed error body

Client components use React Query hooks defined in `lib/api/*.ts`. Each domain file exports typed query and mutation hooks. QueryClient config: `staleTime: 5 min`, `gcTime: 10 min`, no retries on 4xx.

Server API routes (in `app/api/`) call the external backend directly using `fetchJson` with the session `accessToken`.

### Batch processing specifics

Batch file uploads bypass the Next.js proxy and go directly to the backend (`NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL`) with a Bearer token — necessary for large zip files. `useBatchRunStatus()` polls with `refetchInterval` and auto-stops on terminal states. Browser push notifications fire on batch completion.

### Auth

Keycloak OIDC via NextAuth. `lib/auth/options.ts` handles JWT callbacks, realm role extraction (normalized to uppercase), and RP-initiated logout. Session shape: `{ accessToken, idToken, roles }`.

### Environment variables

| Variable                            | Purpose                                               |
| ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL`          | External API base (includes `/api/v1`)                |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRODUCT_API_BASE_URL`  | Product API endpoint                                  |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_BASE_URL` | Workflow engine API endpoint (build-time)             |
| `WORKFLOW_API_KEY`                  | Workflow engine API key (runtime, from K8s secret)    |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_ID`    | Keycloak client                                       |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_KEYCLOAK_ISSUER`       | Keycloak server URL                                   |
| `NEXTAUTH_SECRET`                   | NextAuth secret (also used as Keycloak client secret) |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV`               | Environment identifier (development, qa, production)  |

### Workflow Engine Integration

Workflow engine integration follows the pattern established in mes-genai-ui:

**Configuration:**

- `NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_BASE_URL` — Build-time variable, baked into bundle
- `WORKFLOW_API_KEY` — Runtime variable, injected via Kubernetes secrets

**Environment-specific URLs:**

- Dev: `https://workflow-management-dev.makeitmes.com`
- QA: `https://workflow-management-qa.makeitmes.com`
- Prod: `https://workflow-management.makeitmes.com`

**API Client (`lib/api/workflowApiClient.ts`):**

- Uses Axios with lazy initialization
- API key injected at request time via interceptor (not baked at build)
- Header: `x-api-key`
- Server-side proxy at `app/api/jobs/route.ts` for server-to-server calls

### Multi-Environment Setup

**Build-time environment selection:**

```bash
# Build script selects correct env file
./set-environment.sh [dev|qa|prod]
```

**Environment files:**

- `env/.env.dev` — Development configuration
- `env/.env.qa` — QA configuration
- `env/.env.prod` — Production configuration

The `set-environment.sh` script copies the appropriate env file to `.env.production` during Docker build based on `BUILD_ENVIRONMENT` argument.

### Helm Deployment

**Chart location:** `styls-admin-ui/`

**Key templates:**

- `deployment.yaml` — App deployment with WORKFLOW_API_KEY from secret
- `external-secrets.yaml` — External Secrets Operator config for WORKFLOW_API_KEY
- `tls-external-secret.yaml` — TLS certificate management
- `ingress.yaml` — NGINX ingress configuration

**Secret Management:**

- Uses External Secrets Operator to sync from cloud secret managers (AWS Secrets Manager / GCP Secret Manager)
- `WORKFLOW_API_KEY` synced from `workflow-engine-api-key-{env}` secret
- Refresh interval: 24 hours

**Environment values:**

- `values/values-dev.yaml` — Dev overrides (replicas: 1)
- `values/values-qa.yaml` — QA overrides (replicas: 1)
- `values/values-prod.yaml` — Prod overrides (HPA enabled, node affinity)

content.text:
Next.js 15 (App Router) admin UI for styls.ai. Uses React 19, TanStack Query v5, NextAuth v4 (Keycloak), Tailwind v4, Radix UI, and Sonner for toasts.

### Key directories

- `app/` — Pages and API proxy routes
- `app/api/` — Server-side routes that proxy to external backend with auth
- `lib/api/` — Client-side React Query hooks and types per domain (batch, product, orders, user, workflow)
- `lib/auth/options.ts` — NextAuth Keycloak config; JWT carries `accessToken`, `idToken`, `roles`
- `lib/config.ts` — `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRODUCT_API_BASE_URL` helpers
- `lib/utils/environment.ts` — Environment detection helpers (`isDevelopment()`, `isQAEnvironment()`, `isProduction()`)
- `components/` — Shared UI components built on Radix UI primitives

### Data fetching pattern

All API calls flow through `lib/api/fetcher.ts`:

- `fetcher(url, options)` — returns `Response`; automatically signs out and shows a toast on 401
- `fetchJson<T>(url, options)` — returns `T`; throws with parsed error body

Client components use React Query hooks defined in `lib/api/*.ts`. Each domain file exports typed query and mutation hooks. QueryClient config: `staleTime: 5 min`, `gcTime: 10 min`, no retries on 4xx.

[85 more lines in file. Use offset=40 to continue.]

@@ -49,10 +49,74 @@ Keycloak OIDC via NextAuth. `lib/auth/options.ts` handles JWT callbacks, realm r
 
 ### Environment variables
 
-| Variable                           | Purpose                                               |
-| ---------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
-| `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL`         | External API base (includes `/api/v1`)                |
-| `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRODUCT_API_BASE_URL` | Product API endpoint                                  |
-| `NEXT_PUBLIC_KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_ID`   | Keycloak client                                       |
-| `NEXT_PUBLIC_KEYCLOAK_ISSUER`      | Keycloak server URL                                   |
-| `NEXTAUTH_SECRET`                  | NextAuth secret (also used as Keycloak client secret) |
+| Variable                            | Purpose                                               |
+| ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL`          | External API base (includes `/api/v1`)                |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRODUCT_API_BASE_URL`  | Product API endpoint                                  |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_BASE_URL` | Workflow engine API endpoint (build-time)             |
+| `WORKFLOW_API_KEY`                  | Workflow engine API key (runtime, from K8s secret)    |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_ID`    | Keycloak client                                       |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_KEYCLOAK_ISSUER`       | Keycloak server URL                                   |
+| `NEXTAUTH_SECRET`                   | NextAuth secret (also used as Keycloak client secret) |
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_ENV`               | Environment identifier (development, qa, production)  |
+
+### Workflow Engine Integration
+
+Workflow engine integration follows the pattern established in mes-genai-ui:
+
+**Configuration:**
+
+- `NEXT_PUBLIC_WORKFLOW_API_BASE_URL` — Build-time variable, baked into bundle
+- `WORKFLOW_API_KEY` — Runtime variable, injected via Kubernetes secrets
+
+**Environment-specific URLs:**
+
+- Dev: `https://workflow-management-dev.makeitmes.com`
+- QA: `https://workflow-management-qa.makeitmes.com`
+- Prod: `https://workflow-management.makeitmes.com`
+
+**Server-side proxy at `app/api/jobs/route.ts`:**
+
+- Uses native `fetch` for server-to-server calls to the workflow engine
+- API key passed via `x-api-key` header
+- Client-side code calls `/api/jobs` which proxies to the workflow engine
+
+### Multi-Environment Setup
+
+**Build-time environment selection:**
+
+```bash
+# Build script selects correct env file
+./set-environment.sh [dev|qa|prod]
+```
+
+**Environment files:**
+
+- `env/.env.dev` — Development configuration
+- `env/.env.qa` — QA configuration
+- `env/.env.prod` — Production configuration
+
+The `set-environment.sh` script copies the appropriate env file to `.env.production` during Docker build based on `BUILD_ENVIRONMENT` argument.
+
+### Helm Deployment
+
+**Chart location:** `styls-admin-ui/`
+
+**Key templates:**
+
+- `deployment.yaml` — App deployment with WORKFLOW_API_KEY from secret
+- `external-secrets.yaml` — External Secrets Operator config for WORKFLOW_API_KEY
+- `tls-external-secret.yaml` — TLS certificate management
+- `ingress.yaml` — NGINX ingress configuration
+
+**Secret Management:**
+
+- Uses External Secrets Operator to sync from cloud secret managers (AWS Secrets Manager / GCP Secret Manager)
+- `WORKFLOW_API_KEY` synced from `workflow-engine-api-key-{env}` secret
+- Refresh interval: 24 hours
+
+**Environment values:**
+
+- `values/values-dev.yaml` — Dev overrides (replicas: 1)
+- `values/values-qa.yaml` — QA overrides (replicas: 1)
+- `values/values-prod.yaml` — Prod overrides (HPA enabled, node affinity)

content.text:
**Summary of cleanup changes:**

1. **Deleted** `lib/api/workflowApiClient.ts` — unused Axios client (native `fetch` is used instead via `app/api/jobs/route.ts`)

2. **Updated `AGENTS.md`:**
   - Removed reference to deleted `workflowApiClient.ts` 
   - Removed reference to non-existent `lib/utils/environment.ts`
   - Updated Workflow Engine Integration section to reflect the actual server-side proxy pattern

3. **Cleaned up `lib/api/batch.ts`:**
   - Removed 4 FIXME comments that were outdated development notes

4. **Updated `lib/config.ts`:**
   - Changed FIXME comment to proper documentation comment

**Remaining staged/unstaged changes:**
- Environment files (staged): `.env.dev`, `.env.qa`, `.env.prod`
- Helm chart updates: `deployment.yaml`, `values.yaml`, environment-specific values, new `external-secrets.yaml`
- Config/documentation: `AGENTS.md`, `batch.ts`, `config.ts`

All useless code has been removed. The workflow integration now properly uses:
- Server-side proxy at `app/api/jobs/route.ts` with native `fetch`
- Client-side `useJobs()` hook in `lib/api/batch.ts` calling `/api/jobs`
